title: What is synchronization in Java and why is it important?

answers.junior: Synchronization prevents multiple threads from accessing shared data at the same time.
answers.middle: It ensures thread safety by locking resources, preventing race conditions. Use synchronized keyword or blocks.
answers.senior: Synchronization coordinates thread access to shared resources, avoiding data corruption. Use synchronized methods, blocks, or java.util.concurrent locks. Overuse can cause deadlocks and performance issues.
answers.tricky: Balance synchronization for safety and performance. Use fine-grained locks, java.util.concurrent tools, and avoid deadlocks. Analyze lock contention and prefer immutable objects when possible.
